icchāpakatassa.meaning: of one overcome by desire; of one in whom desire has worked
breakdown: Done away with, put off; affected, overcome (past participle of apakaroti).
icchāpakatassa.note: uncertain: the lexicon parses icchā + apakata, "desire put off / done away with"; in canonical usage icchāpakata commonly means "overcome by desire".

idamavoca.meaning: he spoke this; thus he said
idamavoca.gram: sandhi fusion: neuter pronoun + 3rd person singular aorist of vacati

idampi.gram: sandhi fusion: neuter pronoun + enclitic particle

idampissa.meaning: this too might be (for him); this also would be considered as
idampissa.gram: sandhi fusion: pronoun + particle + 3rd person singular optative
breakdown: Would be (optative of atthi); or, of him (genitive).

idappaccayatāpaccasamuppādo.meaning: conditionality ("having this as its cause") and dependent origination
breakdown: The state of having as condition: idappaccayatā, specific conditionality (the doubled pp is sandhi doubling).
Dependent origination, arising because of a cause (paṭicca, dependent on + samuppāda, arising).
idappaccayatāpaccasamuppādo.note: uncertain: the source spelling -paccasamuppādo appears to be a contraction of paṭiccasamuppādo, dependent origination.

idaṁ.gram: pronoun, neuter nominative/accusative singular of ima

idaṁ thus iddhi.note: uncertain: the key is an artifact of merged vocabulary rows ("idaṁ thus / iddhi strength, power / idha here"); the content belongs under iddhi.

idha.meaning: here; in this world, in this teaching
idha.gram: indeclinable adverb, from the pronoun stem ima

idhupāli.gram: sandhi fusion: adverb + vocative proper noun
breakdown: Upāli (vocative), the monk foremost in the discipline. Sandhi: idha + upāli > idhupāli.

iha.gram: indeclinable adverb, from the pronoun stem ima

indriyānaṁ.gram: neuter noun, genitive/dative plural of indriya
breakdown: Sense faculty; controlling principle (from inda/indra, ruler).

iriyatīti.gram: verb, 3rd person singular present + quotation particle ti
breakdown: Moves, behaves, goes on (√īr); final -i lengthened before ti.
Path, way: iriyāpatha, the postures (walking, standing, sitting, lying).

iriyāpathato.meaning: outside the (four) postures; apart from the modes of deportment
iriyāpathato.gram: masculine compound noun with ablative suffix -to
breakdown: Path, way: the four postures of standing, sitting, walking and lying.

isigaṇaṁ.meaning: a group of sages; a crowd of holy men

issariyakāraṇā.meaning: for the sake of rulership; because of (exercising) supremacy
breakdown: Making, cause; ablative: by reason of.

issariyavossaggena.meaning: by handing over dominion; through relinquishing lordship
breakdown: Handing over, relinquishing (vi + ava + √sajj), instrumental singular.

issarādhipaccaṁ.meaning: the sovereignty of a lord; mastery and supreme power
issarādhipaccaṁ.gram: neuter abstract compound noun, nominative/accusative singular
breakdown: Sovereignty, supreme power (from adhipati, overlord). Sandhi: issara + ādhi- > issarādhi-.

issatta/issattha.gram: neuter noun (issattha, with variant spelling issatta)
breakdown: Craft, science: the craft of the arrow, archery.

issāmacchariyasaṁyojanā.meaning: the fetters of envy and stinginess

issāmanako.meaning: filled with envy; of jealous mind
breakdown: Having a mind of, minded (from mano, mind).

isīti tāpaso..gram: phrase; noun + quotation particle + masculine noun (commentarial gloss)
breakdown: Ascetic, one who practises austerity (from tapa, austerity).

itarā.gram: pronoun/adjective, feminine nominative singular, from ima + -tara

itibhavābhavakathaṁ.meaning: talk of becoming this and not becoming that; chatter about existence and non-existence
breakdown: Non-becoming, non-existence. Sandhi: bhava + abhava > bhavābhava.

iticiticeva.meaning: thus and thus indeed; here and there, this way and that
iticiticeva.gram: sandhi fusion: iti + ca + iti + ca + eva

breakdown: It is said, reportedly: itikirā, hearsay.
itikirāya.goenka: Itikirāya appears in the Kālāma-type advice not to accept a teaching merely on hearsay — the Dhamma is to be verified by one's own direct experience, not accepted on report or rumour.

itipi.meaning: thus also; thus indeed (opening of the Buddha-recollection formula itipi so bhagavā)

itthannāmaṁ.meaning: of such and such a name; so-named
breakdown: Thus, in this way; -ṁ assimilates to -n- before n.

itthattāyā.meaning: for this state of existence; of being in this world
breakdown: Abstract suffix: the state of being thus, this existence.

itthattāyāti.meaning: "...for this state of becoming" (with closing quotation marker, ending the arahant's declaration)
itthattāyāti.gram: neuter abstract noun, dative/genitive singular + quotation particle ti
breakdown: This state of becoming, being in this world (ittha + -tta).
Quotation marker (iti): closes the formula nāparaṁ itthattāyā ti, "there is no more of this state of becoming".

itthaṁ.gram: indeclinable adverb, from the pronoun stem ima

itthidhutto.meaning: a womanizer; one leading a wild life with women
breakdown: Rogue, one leading a wild, dissolute life.

itthikumārikapaṭiggahaṇā.gram: neuter compound action noun, ablative singular

iṭṭhā.gram: past participle of icchati (√is), plural or feminine form
breakdown: Past participle suffix: is + ta > iṭṭha, desired, agreeable.

jagatippadeso.meaning: a spot on the earth; a place or region of the world
breakdown: Spot, place, region (the doubled pp is sandhi doubling).

jagāma.gram: verb, 3rd person singular (reduplicated) aorist/perfect of gacchati (√gam)
jagāma.note: uncertain: the lexicon row conflates this with the neighbouring entry bījagāmabhūtagāmasamārambhā (injury to seeds and plants); jagāma itself is the aorist "went".

jahe.meaning: should abandon; would give up, renounce
jahe.gram: verb, 3rd person singular optative of jahāti (√hā); variant jaheyya
breakdown: Present stem of jahāti, to abandon (√hā).

jahitā.gram: past participle of jahati (√hā), plural or feminine form
breakdown: Present stem of jahati, to abandon (√hā).

jahitā dropped.note: uncertain: the key is an artifact of a split vocabulary row ("jahitā dropped / off, abandoned"); the entry belongs under jahitā.

jambudīpa.meaning: Jambudīpa, the land of the rose-apple (the Indian subcontinent)

janeti.gram: verb, 3rd person singular present, causative of jāyati (√jan)
breakdown: Causative stem of √jan, to be born: to cause to be born, produce.

janetā.meaning: one who brings into being; a producer; a mother
janetā.gram: masculine agent noun from janeti (√jan), nominative singular
breakdown: Causative stem of √jan: to bring into being.
Agent suffix: the one who produces, the genetrix.

jantuno.meaning: of a man; of a person, creature
breakdown: Noun suffix: a born being, creature.

janā.gram: masculine noun, nominative plural of jana (√jan)
breakdown: Root meaning to be born: jana, a person.

jarādhammomhi.meaning: "I am of the nature to age"; I am subject to old age
jarādhammomhi.gram: sandhi fusion: feminine noun + masculine noun + 1st person singular present of atthi
breakdown: I am (1st person singular of atthi). Sandhi: dhammo + amhi > dhammomhi.
jarādhammomhi.goenka: The phrase opens the five subjects for frequent recollection: jarādhammomhi, jaraṁ anatikkanto — "I am of the nature to age, I have not gone beyond ageing." The reflection turns the universal facts of ageing, sickness and death into objects of direct contemplation rather than abstract belief.

jarādhammā.meaning: having the nature of old age; subject to ageing
jarādhammā.gram: compound adjective, plural or feminine form
breakdown: Having the nature of: bound to age.
Ageing, old age (√jar, to grow old).

jayamaṅgalagāthā,.meaning: the Jayamaṅgala Gāthā, the Verses of Blessing and Victory
jayamaṅgalagāthā,.goenka: The Jayamaṅgalagāthā is recited on day four of the 10-day course by S. N. Goenka; the verse referred to here recalls the incident and "attack" caused by Devadatta.
